learning_outcomes.description: Identify the key terminology and concepts developed to systematically investigate social phenomena.
Compare and contrast various theoretical frameworks that have been proposed to account for a social phenomenon.
Evaluate the empirical evidence for various theoretical frameworks.
Apply social psychological constructs/theories in everyday life and to understand ongoing real-world issues or social problems.
Design, plan, and conduct a research study; analyse data and interpret the results.
Develop and write a standard research report, including introduction, method, results, and discussion.

Develop the knowledge and skills that build a capacity in cultural responsiveness to better understand the experiences of underrepresented groups in society, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.

assessments.category: Essay/ Critique, Reflection, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description:
• Students will be asked to complete a short response question during tutorials from Week 3 - 12 (tutors will explain this assessment piece in further detail in the Week 2 tutorial).
  • There will be 8 application questions across 8 tutorials.
  • Each application question is worth 2 marks.
  • At the end of semester, the best 5 (out of 8) attempts will be summed for a mark out of 10.
  • The application question will require students to apply one concept raised in the course materials (including the Cultural Safety Module 2) to a real world problem.
  • Students will get clear guidance in tutorials about the material they need to have reviewed prior to the application question the following week.
  • There is no alternative assessment for the application questions.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Each application question will be completed during tutorials.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
• The Media Diary assessment will test your understanding of the concepts and theories covered in lecture. 
  • Each week, you will be tasked with finding one piece of media that demonstrates any concept/theory covered in lecture that week.
  • You will be required to identify and explain the specific concept/theory that is reflected in the media piece you have selected.
  • You will record this analysis in a media diary, which you will submit at the end of semester for marking.
  • During tutorials throughout the semester, tutors can give verbal feedback to let you know whether you are on the right track.
  • Further details will be provided on Blackboard (Learn.UQ).
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment should be submitted online using Turnitin. Electronic checks will be conducted to identify instances of plagiarism and collusion.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
• This assignment involves writing a research report (containing Abstract, Introduction, Method, Results, Discussion, and Reference section) on an assigned topic.
  • The research project will be developed and explained in a series of tutorials over the course of the semester. Students are directly involved in contributing ideas and collecting data for the project.
  • Tutorial attendance is essential to understanding the research project and how to write up the research report.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment should be submitted online using Turnitin. Electronic checks will be conducted to identify instances of plagiarism and collusion.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
• The exam is administered by UQ Central Exams and will be scheduled during the University examination period.
  • The exam will consist of multiple choice questions. 
  • The questions will assess content presented in lectures across the entire semester (Week 1 to Week 12).
  • More information will be provided over the course of the semester.
  • If, owing to exceptional circumstances, you are unable to sit the final exam, you must complete an online application on mySi-net. The full details can be found in the School of Psychology Student Guidelines of this profile. Please note that the Course Coordinator and Course Administrator in the School cannot authorise deferred exams and have no control over these decisions: The application is assessed by university-wide central exam administrators.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Cultural Safety Module 2

A self-paced online module supporting the development of cultural safety skills and awareness will be made available to you on Blackboard.
learning_activities.topic_description: Cultural Safety Module 2 A self-paced online module supporting the development of cultural safety skills and awareness will be made available to you on Blackboard.

learning_activities.topic: Mid-semester break - No tutorial
learning_activities.topic_description: Mid-semester break - No tutorial

learning_activities.topic: Mid-semester break - No lecture
learning_activities.topic_description: Mid-semester break - No lecture

learning_activities.topic:
Course Introduction

This lecture will provide an overview of the course and an introduction to the different methodologies used in social and organisational psychology.
learning_activities.topic_description: Course Introduction This lecture will provide an overview of the course and an introduction to the different methodologies used in social and organisational psychology.

learning_activities.topic:
The Self

This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about the self. For example, how do we think about ourselves and how does this influence our attitudes and behaviours?
learning_activities.topic_description: The Self This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about the self. For example, how do we think about ourselves and how does this influence our attitudes and behaviours?

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial Introduction

This tutorial provides on overview of the tutorial program and key assessment pieces, and a brief introduction to the topic of the Research Report.
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial Introduction This tutorial provides on overview of the tutorial program and key assessment pieces, and a brief introduction to the topic of the Research Report.

learning_activities.topic:
Social Cognition

This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about social cognition. For example, what factors shape the judgements we make of others?
learning_activities.topic_description: Social Cognition This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about social cognition. For example, what factors shape the judgements we make of others?

learning_activities.topic:
Attitudes

This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about attitudes. For example, what are attitudes, and how do we come to hold attitudes about a variety of issues, topics, and things?
learning_activities.topic_description: Attitudes This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about attitudes. For example, what are attitudes, and how do we come to hold attitudes about a variety of issues, topics, and things?

learning_activities.topic:
Report: Overview & Brainstorming

The first of a series of tutorials to help you prepare for the Research Report assignment. This tutorial will introduce you to the research project and give you an opportunity to work together to suggest variables you would like to investigate as part of the project. Here, you will be directly contributing to the design of the study. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The first application question will be administered this week.
learning_activities.topic_description: Report: Overview & Brainstorming The first of a series of tutorials to help you prepare for the Research Report assignment. This tutorial will introduce you to the research project and give you an opportunity to work together to suggest variables you would like to investigate as part of the project. Here, you will be directly contributing to the design of the study. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The first application question will be administered this week.

learning_activities.topic:
Attitude Change

This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about attitude change. For example, what factors contribute to attitude change and what prevents us from changing our attitudes?
learning_activities.topic_description: Attitude Change This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about attitude change. For example, what factors contribute to attitude change and what prevents us from changing our attitudes?

learning_activities.topic:
Research Report: Hypotheses

This tutorial will outline the variables to be included in the final research project. You will be asked to work together to formulate hypotheses for each of these variables. This tutorial will also cover the information you will need to include in the Introduction section of your Research Report. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The second application question will be administered this week.
learning_activities.topic_description: Research Report: Hypotheses This tutorial will outline the variables to be included in the final research project. You will be asked to work together to formulate hypotheses for each of these variables. This tutorial will also cover the information you will need to include in the Introduction section of your Research Report. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The second application question will be administered this week.

learning_activities.topic:
Social Influence

This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about social influence. For example, why does the presence of an audience change our behaviours, and how do factors like social norms and power influence our behaviours?
learning_activities.topic_description: Social Influence This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about social influence. For example, why does the presence of an audience change our behaviours, and how do factors like social norms and power influence our behaviours?

learning_activities.topic:
Research Report: Research Method

This tutorial will cover the information you will need to include in the Method section of your Research Report. Participant recruitment procedures for the research project will be explained to you so that you can assist in data collection. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The third application question will be administered this week.
learning_activities.topic_description: Research Report: Research Method This tutorial will cover the information you will need to include in the Method section of your Research Report. Participant recruitment procedures for the research project will be explained to you so that you can assist in data collection. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The third application question will be administered this week.

learning_activities.topic:
Groups & Belonging

This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about groups and our belonging to groups. For example, what is the effect of not belonging to a group, and how do groups influence us?
learning_activities.topic_description: Groups & Belonging This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about groups and our belonging to groups. For example, what is the effect of not belonging to a group, and how do groups influence us?

learning_activities.topic:
Report: Introduction

This tutorial will focus on how to write the Introduction section of your Research Report.You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The fourth application question will be administered this week.
learning_activities.topic_description: Report: Introduction This tutorial will focus on how to write the Introduction section of your Research Report.You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The fourth application question will be administered this week.

learning_activities.topic:
Prejudice

This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about prejudice. For example, what are the different forms of prejudice, and how do people come to hold prejudice?
learning_activities.topic_description: Prejudice This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about prejudice. For example, what are the different forms of prejudice, and how do people come to hold prejudice?

learning_activities.topic:
Research Report: Results & Discussion

You will be presented with the results from our research project (exciting!) and your tutor will guide you through how to interpret the results. This tutorial will also cover the information you will need to include in the Results and Discussion sections of your Research Report. By the end of this tutorial, you will have all the information you need to write up the bulk of your Research Report. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The fifth application question will be administered this week.
learning_activities.topic_description: Research Report: Results & Discussion You will be presented with the results from our research project (exciting!) and your tutor will guide you through how to interpret the results. This tutorial will also cover the information you will need to include in the Results and Discussion sections of your Research Report. By the end of this tutorial, you will have all the information you need to write up the bulk of your Research Report. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The fifth application question will be administered this week.

learning_activities.topic:
Prejudice Reduction

This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about prejudice reduction. For example, what strategies can be used to reduce prejudice, and how effective are they?
learning_activities.topic_description: Prejudice Reduction This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about prejudice reduction. For example, what strategies can be used to reduce prejudice, and how effective are they?

learning_activities.topic:
Research Report: Finishing Touches

This tutorial will cover the APA 7th formatting rules that your final report should adhere to as well as last minutes tips as you prepare your research report for submission the following week. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The sixth application question will be administered this week.
learning_activities.topic_description: Research Report: Finishing Touches This tutorial will cover the APA 7th formatting rules that your final report should adhere to as well as last minutes tips as you prepare your research report for submission the following week. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The sixth application question will be administered this week.

learning_activities.topic:
Antisocial & Prosocial Behaviour

This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about antisocial and prosocial behaviours. For example, when and why do people behave in cruel and kind ways?
learning_activities.topic_description: Antisocial & Prosocial Behaviour This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about antisocial and prosocial behaviours. For example, when and why do people behave in cruel and kind ways?

learning_activities.topic:
Wrongdoing & Forgiveness

This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about wrongdoing and forgiveness. For example, what does it take to repair a relationship after a wrongdoing has occurred?
learning_activities.topic_description: Wrongdoing & Forgiveness This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about wrongdoing and forgiveness. For example, what does it take to repair a relationship after a wrongdoing has occurred?

learning_activities.topic:
Assessment Check-in

Tutors will check in and provide guidance on the Media Diary. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The seventh application question will be administered this week.
learning_activities.topic_description: Assessment Check-in Tutors will check in and provide guidance on the Media Diary. You will engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The seventh application question will be administered this week.

learning_activities.topic:
Love & Attraction

This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about love and attraction. For example, why are we attracted to others, and what factors shape relationship satisfaction?
learning_activities.topic_description: Love & Attraction This lecture will cover theories, concepts, and research about love and attraction. For example, why are we attracted to others, and what factors shape relationship satisfaction?

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial Finale

This will be the final scheduled tutorial. Students will learn about post-graduate pathways in psychology. You will also engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The eighth application question will be administered this week.
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial Finale This will be the final scheduled tutorial. Students will learn about post-graduate pathways in psychology. You will also engage in a lecture-related activity/discussion. The eighth application question will be administered this week.

learning_activities.topic:
Revision

This lecture will focus on revision and will cover practice exam questions to help students prepare for the final exam.
learning_activities.topic_description: Revision This lecture will focus on revision and will cover practice exam questions to help students prepare for the final exam.
